data.content:
Here's my analysis of the legacy patterns and the modernized SDK-style equivalent:

## Legacy Patterns Identified

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Replacement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace | `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` attribute |
| 2 | Manual `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 4 | `Configuration`/`Platform` conditional PropertyGroups | Defaults handled by SDK |
| 5 | `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK defaults |
| 6 | Explicit `<Compile Include="..." />` items | Automatic file globbing |
| 7 | `<Reference Include="System" />` etc. | Implicit framework references |
| 8 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Project-level properties (`GenerateAssemblyInfo`) |
| 9 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | `TargetFramework` shorthand (`net472` or `net8.0`) |
| 10 | Debug/Release `OutputPath`, `DefineConstants` | SDK defaults |

Now I'll apply the modernization:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

arguments.description: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove obsolete AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **XML namespace and ToolsVersion** - `ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003"` → replaced by `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
2. **Manual Import of Microsoft.Common.props and Microsoft.CSharp.targets** → implicit in SDK-style
3. **Configuration/Platform PropertyGroups** - Debug/Release conditions with default values → most are defaults in SDK-style


4. **ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, AutoGenerateBindingRedirects, and Deterministic** → all either unnecessary or default in SDK-style projects
5. **Explicit Compile ItemGroup** → SDK-style automatically includes all .cs files through globbing patterns
6. **Framework references like System and System.Core** → no longer needed since they're implicit when targeting .NET Framework or not required for .NET Core/5+
7. **Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs** → can be consolidated into project-level properties using GenerateAssemblyInfo
8. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** → I'll convert this to `net472` in the new format, or modernize it to `net8.0` for current .NET

Now I'm going to modernize this to SDK-style targeting .NET 8, eliminate the separate AssemblyInfo.cs file by moving its metadata into the project file itself, and strip away all the unnecessary boilerplate.

data.content:
**Done.** The project went from 47 lines → 13 lines. Here's what was removed/simplified:

- **Imports & targets** → replaced by `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
- **Debug/Release PropertyGroups** → SDK provides sensible defaults
- **`<Compile>` items** → automatic globbing includes all `.cs` files
- **Framework references** (System, System.Core, etc.) → implicit in .NET 8
- **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** → metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (auto-generated at build)
- **ProjectGuid, FileAlignment, Deterministic, etc.** → unnecessary/default in SDK-style
- **Target framework** upgraded from .NET Framework 4.7.2 → .NET 8.0
